title: Tectaria wigmanii
intro: Tectaria wigmanii, also known as Wigman's fern or Tectaria fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Tectaria wigmanii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Tectaria wigmanii is often prepared as a decoction or tea, or used in the form of a topical cream or ointment to take advantage of its therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Tectaria wigmanii is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This fern belongs to the genus Tectaria, which consists of various species of ferns native to tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Tectaria wigmanii further breaks down into the kingdom Plantae, and the phylum and division both being Pteridophyta and Polypodiopsida, respectively.
